steps.title: Identify the subject and its number
steps.content: "The sculpture" is the subject — singular. The context describes its current location, so the verb should be present tense.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content:
Rule: a singular subject takes a singular verb. Present tense for a permanent state uses simple present.
  • Choice A — "were standing": plural past progressive — wrong number and wrong tense for a permanent present location. ✗
  • Choice B — "have been standing": plural present perfect — "have" is plural and does not agree with singular "the sculpture". ✗
  • Choice C — "stands": singular simple present — agrees with "the sculpture" and correctly describes its permanent location. ✓
  • Choice D — "are standing": plural present progressive — "are" is plural, violating agreement with the singular subject. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
